extra.message: User-controlled URL parameter is passed directly to urllib.request.urlopen(). The startsWith check on 'https://insecurewebapp.com' is trivially bypassable (e.g., https://insecurewebapp.com.evil.com), allowing Server-Side Request Forgery to internal or external hosts.

User-controlled request path is concatenated to os.getcwd() and opened with open(path).read(). The weak regex check only looks for '/../../' at the start of the path, allowing trivial path traversal bypasses (e.g., /foo/../../../etc/passwd) to read arbitrary files.

cwe: CWE-22: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory

extra.message: The decrypted username value from the session cookie is written directly into the HTTP response body without HTML escaping. An attacker can craft a malicious cookie (or bit-flip a legitimate one in AES-CBC mode) to inject arbitrary HTML/JavaScript, resulting in reflected XSS.

The sign_for_payment function computes a signature using hashlib.sha256(secret + data) instead of HMAC. This construction is vulnerable to length extension attacks, allowing attackers to forge valid signatures for modified payment data.

cwe: CWE-353: Missing Support for Integrity Check

extra.message: The sign_for_payment function computes a signature using hashlib.sha256(secret + data) instead of HMAC. This construction is vulnerable to length extension attacks, allowing attackers to forge valid signatures for modified payment data.

cwe: CWE-353: Missing Support for Integrity Check

extra.message: ssl.SSLContext.check_hostname is explicitly set to False, disabling server hostname verification during the TLS handshake. This allows the client to accept certificates for any hostname, making it vulnerable to man-in-the-middle attacks.

cwe: CWE-297: Improper Validation of Certificate with Host Mismatch

extra.message: The Flask SECRET_KEY is hardcoded as 'qwerty12345'. This weak, hardcoded secret can be used to forge session cookies and bypass authentication or authorization checks.
The Flask SECRET_KEY is hardcoded as '55555'. This weak, hardcoded secret can be used to forge session cookies and bypass authentication or authorization checks.
The HTTP server is bound to '0.0.0.0', exposing the application on all network interfaces. This increases the attack surface by making the service reachable from any network interface.

cwe: CWE-1327: Binding to an Unrestricted IP Address
